seoTitle: Fielding vs Fifteen Mile — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fielding and Fifteen Mile. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fielding vs Fifteen Mile: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fielding and Fifteen Mile.
heading: Fielding vs Fifteen Mile — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fielding, Queensland and Fifteen Mile, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fifteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Fielding (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fielding or Fifteen Mile safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fielding has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Fifteen Mile scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fielding scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Fifteen Mile scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fielding or Fifteen Mile?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fifteen Mile has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area face minimal climate value impact based on current projections.
